commit.message:
feat: add fun declaration and return statement (#25)

* feat: add fun declaration and return statement

- Add FunctionType enum (SCRIPT / FUNCTION) to Compiler
- Compiler stores m_type and m_enclosing for nested compilation
- funDeclaration(): parse fun <name>(<params>) { <body> } at any
  declaration site; supports global and local scope
- parseFunction(): inner Compiler compiles params + body into a fresh
  ObjFunction; uses beginScope() so params live at depth 1; RETURN
  cleans the frame without needing an explicit endScope()
- returnStatement(): compile error if called at script level;
  return; emits NIL+RETURN; return <expr>; emits expr+RETURN
- declaration() dispatches on TokenType::FUN
- statement() dispatches on TokenType::RETURN
- Add compile_fn_body_to_bytecode() test helper to inspect inner
  function chunks
- Add FunctionBytecodeTest: empty body, params+return, local var
- Add FunctionTest: return nil, return value, return with args,
  arity errors, recursion, two functions, local function,
  stack-clean invariant, top-level return compile error

fix: Complete responsive layout for report title/subtitle inputs

Make summary header layout fully responsive:
- Change flex container from 'items-center justify-between' to 'flex-col gap-2 sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between'
- Make margin responsive: 'mr-4' → 'sm:mr-4' (no margin on extra-small)

This ensures title and subtitle inputs get full width on ultra-narrow screens
(< 640px breakpoint) while maintaining horizontal layout on larger screens.

On screens under 320px width (extra small), inputs now stack vertically above
the controls, giving them full width and preventing text field truncation.

fix: Make report title/subtitle inputs responsive on ultra-narrow screens

horizontally on larger screens using responsive Tailwind classes:
- Change 'flex items-center justify-between' to 'flex flex-col gap-2 sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between'

(< 320px width) where the right side controls were taking up too much space.

On small screens, inputs stack above the controls. On larger screens (sm+),
they layout horizontally as before.

commit.message:
fix: address security vulnerabilities in unsafe memory access and XML parsing (#82)

- Mitigate XXE attacks by disabling DTD processing and external entity
  resolution when loading XML schemas (SBESourceGenerator.cs)
- Break on failed TryRead in group consumption loop to prevent runaway
  reads from malicious NumInGroup values (MessageDefinition.cs)
- Validate blockLength against buffer.Length before Slice in TryParse
  backward compatibility path (MessageDefinition.cs)
- Use checked arithmetic in SumFieldLength to detect integer overflow
  from malicious schema field lengths (FileContentGeneratorExtensions.cs)
- Add SBE007 diagnostic warning when schema specifies non-native byte
  order, since the generator does not emit endianness conversion logic
- Update snapshot tests to reflect new bounds check in generated code

commit.message:
fix: rename 'Model Calls' to 'API Requests' in shutdown-cycles table (#863)

The shutdown-cycles detail table displayed a column labelled 'Model Calls'
whose values were actually API request counts from the session.shutdown
payload. This conflicted with the summary table's 'Model Calls' column
which shows turn-start event counts — a different metric.

- Checker populates assign_target_kinds in TypeCheckOutput for each

  FieldAccess, or Index.

- C++ ast_types.h and msgpack_reader.cpp updated to parse the new field.
- MLIRGenStmt.cpp generateAssignStmt now calls requireAssignTargetKindOf
  at entry, failing closed instead of falling through ad-hoc branches.
- Focused tests: checker side-table population and msgpack wire/builder
  coverage; C++ build remains gated on the unavailable cmake/ninja toolchain.

Refs: assign-target-authority slice PR A

* Sync generated msgpack reader

* Validate assign target authority metadata

* Parse assign_target_kinds in msgpack reader

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check trait default method bodies for assign_target_kinds

Type checker skipped Item::Trait in check_item, leaving assign_target_kinds
unpopulated for assignments inside trait default method bodies.  MLIR codegen
uses requireAssignTargetKindOf (fail-closed) for every assignment, so the
missing entry caused a hard error on the bump() default in
param_mutability_codegen.hew.

Fix: add check_trait_defaults, which constructs a synthetic FnDecl for each
default method body and runs check_function_as on it.  This binds params into
env so Expr::Identifier targets resolve to LocalVar and get an entry in the
assign_target_kinds side-table.

Also add assign_target_kinds as the 7th required field in
packWithSchema / drop_funcs_roundtrip / method_call_receiver_kinds_roundtrip
in test_msgpack_reader.cpp; parseMsgpackAST uses mapReq so any minimal-valid
program fixture must include it.

that single P2P ops hang. Although we expect oneCCL to support this, a
workaround is to add a group around send/recv ops if there is no current
active group.

commit.message:
r600: add Terakan Shader ABI Contract (Phase 3 design)

Formal hardware resource allocation map for TeraScale-2 Vulkan driver,
validated by two independent rubber-duck reviews:

- KCACHE bank map: 0-13 UBOs, 14 robustness metadata, 15 push constants
- Write robustness via dedicated bank 14 buffer (not push constants)
  avoids ABI break, cache invalidation, and bank 15 indexing restriction
- Read robustness is zero-cost (hardware-enforced by VTX/TEX units)
- Saturating bounds check guards ALL MEM_RAT ops (stores + atomics)
- 4-phase pass discipline: resource-agnostic → layout → ABI → cleanup
- System value 3-tier folding: compile-time → layout-derived → runtime
- Complete resource/sampler/UAV slot budgets from header analysis

Findings from EVERGREEN_OOB_ROBUSTNESS_FINDINGS.md (Phase 5 probes)
drive the read/write asymmetry that makes this architecture viable.

commit.message:
Adding "winapp ui" for UI automation (#380)

## Description

Adds a new winapp ui command group that enables UI automation for
Windows apps using Microsoft UI Automation (UIA). This allows
developers and AI agents to programmatically discover, inspect, and
interact with UI elements across any Windows application.

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| ---------------- |
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| ui list-windows | List visible windows for an app |
| ui status | Connect to an app and print session info |
| ui inspect | Dump the UI tree (or ancestors/subtree) |
| ui search | Find elements by text query |
| ui invoke | Activate an element via UIA patterns (Invoke, Toggle,
Select, ExpandCollapse) |
| ui click | Click via mouse input at screen coordinates (fallback for
non-invocable controls) |
| ui focus | Move keyboard focus to an element |
| ui set-value | Set text/value on editable controls |
| ui get-property | Read UIA property values from an element |
| ui get-focused | Report the currently focused element |
| ui screenshot | Save a PNG of a window or element |
| ui scroll | Scroll a container (up/down/left/right/top/bottom) |
| ui scroll-into-view | Scroll a target element into view |
| ui wait-for | Wait for element appearance, disappearance, or property
change |

## Key design decisions

- Semantic slug selectors — inspect and search output stable, shell-safe
element slugs (e.g., btn-close-d1a2) that can be passed
 directly to other commands
- Flexible app targeting — Target apps by process name, window title,
PID, or HWND (--window for stability)
- Invokable ancestor fallback — search and invoke can surface/use a
parent element that supports invoke when the matched child
does not
- Mouse input fallback — click uses real mouse injection (SetCursorPos +
SendInput) for controls that don't expose UIA invoke
patterns
- Testable architecture — UIA and session services are behind interfaces
with fakes for unit testing

## Example usage

 winapp ui inspect --app notepad
 winapp ui search "Save" --app myapp
 winapp ui invoke btn-save-d1a2 --app myapp
 winapp ui screenshot --app myapp --output screen.png
 winapp ui wait-for btn-done-f3c1 --app myapp --timeout 10

## Type of Change

<!-- Keep the applicable line(s), delete the rest -->

- ✨ New feature

- [x] Main [README.md](../README.md) updated (if applicable)

changed)

applicable)

commit.message:
Add locks to buildtrees and packages. (#1979)

This is a followup on https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg-tool/pull/1938
Resolves
https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/pull/51004#issuecomment-4195577101

@autoantwort points out that our MSBuild integration leaves the default
buildtrees location and only overrides the installed root:


https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/blob/81e3c888ef717a026eb57bb2f3cb0dbd30246271/scripts/buildsystems/msbuild/vcpkg.targets#L183-L190

I initially tried a solution which would get `buildtrees()` out of
`VcpkgPaths` entirely and pass around something that had both the data
and effectively encapsulated that things had been locked, but changes to
make that happen were starting to get huge, and I saw that I had already
accidentally created deadlocks by being inconsistent about the order of
taking the lock on the installed tree vs. buildtrees and packages, so I
gave up on it. (
https://github.com/BillyONeal/vcpkg-tool/commit/0a4b9ef757f68fd784bc14994bb7fb85ecd182eb
)
